metadata.notes: Backend routes significantly exceed spec endpoints — engines contain many USP-slug and P2/P3 extended routes beyond core spec. API-client coverage is nearly complete; 4 known DELETE gaps documented below.

routes.spec_desc: Validate and route tool calls securely
AI use disclosure and mode labeling
Publish hiring event to HR stack

duplicates.resolution: Each engine serves /overrides at its own base path (/api/analytics/overrides, /api/compliance/overrides, /api/ai/overrides) — paths are unique at the router level but the relative slug collides conceptually. Recommend aliasing to fully-qualified paths and deprecating slug shorthand.
duplicates.impact: Ambiguous routing if a shared express app mounts engines without base-path prefixes

duplicates.resolution: Two separate base paths — functionally duplicated business logic. Sourcing engine owns submission; candidate-hub duplicates. Consolidate to sourcing-engine and add a thin proxy in candidate-hub.
duplicates.impact: Data may be written twice; referral counts inflated

duplicates.resolution: Exact same route defined twice within mobility/routes.ts. The second definition at line 2445 silently shadows the first. Remove the duplicate at line 2445.
duplicates.impact: Express uses first-match routing — line 2445 handler is unreachable dead code

duplicates.resolution: Sourcing engine is the canonical source. Candidate-hub endpoint should proxy or be removed.
duplicates.impact: Inconsistent data if both endpoints query different tables

duplicates.resolution: Sourcing engine is the canonical owner. Candidate-hub endpoint should be a view/proxy.
duplicates.impact: Silver medalist tagging logic split across engines risks divergence

duplicates.resolution: Decision engine owns the resource; explainability engine duplicates for convenience. Explainability engine should call decision engine internally or use a shared service.
duplicates.impact: Possible divergent payloads returned for the same decision ID

missing_from_api_client.spec_desc: Archive requisition — implemented in routes.ts:256, present in spec, but api-client only exposes archiveRequisition via del() which appears as a stub referencing the same path in the platform section. Verify the del() call is wired up; if not, add: archiveRequisition: (id) => del(`/requisitions/${id}`)

Revoke consent — implemented in security-framework/routes.ts:162, in spec, but del() call not present in api.security object. Add: revokeConsent: (candidateId) => del(`/security/consent/${candidateId}`)
Purge vault entry — implemented in security-framework/routes.ts:455, in spec, but del() call not present in api.security object. Add: purgeVaultEntry: (id) => del(`/security/vault/${id}`)

Release legal hold — implemented in compliance-governance/routes.ts:303, in spec, but del() call not present in api.compliance object. Add: releaseLegalHold: (id) => del(`/compliance/legal-hold/${id}`)

ui_route_coverage.notes: build_spec.json reports 358 fully_functional + 14 engine_level features. Engine-level features do not map to individual UI routes — they are surfaced via their parent engine dashboards. All 358 fully_functional features have ui_route entries in the spec.

missing_pages.issue: engine-level feature — no dedicated UI page, surfaces via /mobility/* sub-routes
engine-level feature — no dedicated UI page, surfaces via /onboarding/* sub-routes
engine-level feature — no dedicated UI page

missing_pages.feature_name: Unified ATS + CRM + scheduling + analytics on a single data model
missing_pages.issue: slug_mismatch — spec generates /platform/unified-ats-+-crm-+-scheduling-+-analytics-on-a-single-data- (truncated slug with special chars)
slug_mismatch — backend route truncates to /analytics/candidate-journey-analytics-with-drop-off-point-identificati (64-char Express segment limit)
